column1: Your right of withdrawal: under Art. 9 you have 14 days to withdraw from this distance contract without giving a reason. For physical goods, the 14-day period starts on the day you (or a third party nominated by you) take physical possession of the goods. For services and digital content, different starting dates apply (Art. 9(2)(a)/(c)).

For physical goods, the 14-day period starts on the day you (or a third party nominated by you) take physical possession of the goods. For services and digital content, different starting dates apply (Art. 9(2)(a)/(c)).

column1: Pick one or more order statuses whose transition means the customer received the goods in your fulfillment workflow. The 14-day withdrawal window starts on the timestamp of the order's earliest transition into any of the selected statuses (Art. 9(2)(b) Directive 2011/83/EU). Multi-select supports merchants who use several "delivered"-type statuses (e.g. delivered + complete + delivered_partial). Until the order reaches any of them, the right of withdrawal is open with no fixed deadline.
column2: Pick one or more order statuses whose transition means the customer received the goods in your fulfillment workflow. The 14-day withdrawal window starts on the timestamp of the order's earliest transition into any of the selected statuses (Art. 9(2)(b) Directive 2011/83/EU). Multi-select supports merchants who use several "delivered"-type statuses (e.g. delivered + complete + delivered_partial). Until the order reaches any of them, the right of withdrawal is open with no fixed deadline.

column1: Excludes products made to customer specifications or clearly personalised (e.g. engraved jewellery, made-to-measure clothing). Requires product attribute is_custom_made = true. If enabled, orders containing such items cannot be withdrawn — customers will see this when attempting to start withdrawal.
column2: Excludes products made to customer specifications or clearly personalised (e.g. engraved jewellery, made-to-measure clothing). Requires product attribute is_custom_made = true. If enabled, orders containing such items cannot be withdrawn — customers will see this when attempting to start withdrawal.

column1: Excludes goods that can deteriorate or expire rapidly (fresh food, cut flowers, short-shelf-life pharmaceuticals). Requires product attribute is_perishable = true. Do not enable for all categories — apply it only to truly perishable SKUs, otherwise you will unlawfully deny valid withdrawals.
column2: Excludes goods that can deteriorate or expire rapidly (fresh food, cut flowers, short-shelf-life pharmaceuticals). Requires product attribute is_perishable = true. column1: Excludes sealed hygiene or health items only if the seal has been broken after delivery (cosmetics, intimate apparel, sealed medical supplies). column2: Excludes sealed hygiene or health items only if the seal has been broken after delivery (cosmetics, intimate apparel, sealed medical supplies). column1: Excludes sealed audio recordings, video recordings, or sealed computer software only if the seal has been broken after delivery. Requires product attribute is_sealed_av = true. column1: Two-stage GDPR data lifecycle for the mm_eu_withdrawal_request table. Withdrawal requests carry both personally identifiable data (customer name, email, IP, user agent, admin notes, denial reasons) and a content hash that is the Art. 11a(4) durable-medium legal proof. The two have different mandatory retention periods, so we store them on the same row and age them out separately.
column2: Two-stage GDPR data lifecycle for the mm_eu_withdrawal_request table. Withdrawal requests carry both personally identifiable data (customer name, email, IP, user agent, admin notes, denial reasons) and a content hash that is the Art. 11a(4) durable-medium legal proof. The two have different mandatory retention periods, so we store them on the same row and age them out separately.

column1:
How long the personally identifiable columns stay on a row before the daily cron nullifies them: customer_name, customer_email, customer_ip, user_agent, denial_reason, admin_note. The row itself stays — only PII is wiped. Counted from the row's created_at.

Default: 730 days (2 years). This is the floor — GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) ("storage limitation") balanced against the statute of limitations for consumer-rights claims in most EU member states (1–2 years). Range: 730–3650 days.
column2:
How long the personally identifiable columns stay on a row before the daily cron nullifies them: customer_name, customer_email, customer_ip, user_agent, denial_reason, admin_note. The row itself stays — only PII is wiped. Counted from the row's created_at.

Default: 730 days (2 years). This is the floor — GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) ("storage limitation") balanced against the statute of limitations for consumer-rights claims in most EU member states (1–2 years). Range: 730–3650 days.

column1:
How long the content hash (Art. 11a(4) durable-medium proof) stays. After PII has been wiped above, the row keeps only the SHA-256 hash + receipt timestamps until this period expires; then the cron deletes the whole row.

Default: 2190 days (6 years). Matches the longest commercial-records retention obligation in EU member states (typically 6 years for accounting / VAT). Must be ≥ PII Retention. Range: 2190–3650 days.
column2:
How long the content hash (Art. 11a(4) durable-medium proof) stays. After PII has been wiped above, the row keeps only the SHA-256 hash + receipt timestamps until this period expires; then the cron deletes the whole row.

Default: 2190 days (6 years). Matches the longest commercial-records retention obligation in EU member states (typically 6 years for accounting / VAT). Must be ≥ PII Retention. Range: 2190–3650 days.

column1:
Append-only audit log of all withdrawal lifecycle events (GDPR Art. 5(2) accountability).
                    Personal data (IP, user agent, customer actor ID) is automatically nullified after the retention period.
                    The audit rows themselves are never deleted — only anonymised.
column2:
Append-only audit log of all withdrawal lifecycle events (GDPR Art. 5(2) accountability).
                    Personal data (IP, user agent, customer actor ID) is automatically nullified after the retention period.
                    The audit rows themselves are never deleted — only anonymised.

column1: Art. 16(m) CRD exclusion — digital content where the customer has (1) given express prior consent to begin performance and (2) acknowledged that they lose the withdrawal right. Two switches: the Detection subgroup decides what counts as digital (master switch), the Checkout step + Email subgroup renders the 2-checkbox waiver UX and dispatches the confirmation email. Both must be ON for the feature to work end-to-end. Use this only for one-time digital purchases (ebooks, software installers, music files, games). Do not enable it for ongoing digital subscriptions or services (SaaS, streaming, hosted software) — EU case law (CJEU Sky, C-641/19) requires a different consent flow for those.
